title: textscan
summary: Parse formatted text from a string or file identifier.

gpu_support.notes: Runs on the CPU. Formatted text parsing consumes host text and returns host cell arrays.

description: `C = textscan(textOrFileID, formatSpec)` parses formatted text from a string or from the current position of a file identifier opened by `fopen`.
behaviors: Accepts string, string-array scalar, row character-vector, numeric file identifier, or scalar numeric tensor file identifier as the first argument.
Supports optional repeat count `N` before name-value options.
Supports numeric conversions `%f`, `%e`, `%g`, `%d`, `%i`, and `%u`; text conversions `%s`, `%q`, `%c`, and character-set conversions such as `%[ABC]` and `%[^,]`.
Supports skipped conversions with `%*` and field widths such as `%5f` or `%10s`.
Text columns return cell columns of strings. Numeric columns return column tensors. With `'CollectOutput', true`, adjacent numeric columns are grouped into one numeric matrix.
Line comments and block comments are ignored while scanning input. Blank records are skipped after header/comment processing.
`ReturnOnError` defaults to true, returning data parsed before the first incompatible token. Set it to false to raise a parse error.

examples.description: Parse CSV with header and missing tokens

faqs.question: Does `textscan` read filenames directly?
faqs.answer: No. Like MATLAB, a text string is parsed as text content. Use `fopen` to get a file identifier when reading a file.

faqs.question: How are text outputs represented?
faqs.answer: Text conversions return cell columns containing string values. Numeric conversions return dense double tensors.

faqs.question: When should I use `readmatrix` instead?
faqs.answer: Use `readmatrix` for simple numeric delimited files. Use `textscan` when legacy code has explicit format strings, mixed text/numeric columns, comments, or skipped fields.

gpu_residency: `textscan` is a host file/string import source. It returns host cells and tensors and does not create GPU-resident arrays.
gpu_behavior: `textscan` does not launch provider kernels or participate in fusion.
